example.code:
# pyproject.toml [tool.poetry] name = "acme" [tool.poetry.dependencies] python = "^3.11" fastapi = "^0.110"
[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies] ruff = "^0.4"
[tool.poetry.group.test.dependencies] pytest = "^8"
[tool.poetry.group.docs] optional = true [tool.poetry.group.docs.dependencies] mkdocs = "^1.5"
# CI step - run: poetry install --sync --no-interaction
# Dockerfile FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder RUN pip install poetry COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./ RUN poetry export -f requirements.txt -o /tmp/req.txt --without-hashes
FROM python:3.11-slim COPY --from=builder /tmp/req.txt . RUN pip install -r req.txt

# pyproject.toml — REJECTED python = ">=3.8" # r5 violated # poetry.lock in .gitignore # r1 violated
# Dockerfile FROM python:3.11 COPY . . RUN pip install poetry && poetry install # r7 violated
example.note: 3 violations: broad Python range, no lockfile, Poetry in final stage.

list.item: Dep groups main/dev/test (and docs optional) declared.
Dockerfile is multi-stage; final stage uses pip + requirements.txt.
CI caches .venv keyed by OS+Python+poetry.lock hash.
